name: Repurpose content into 5-platform posts with Groq and Upload-Post

nodes.name: Check if input is URL or text

parameters.content:
## Repurpose content into 5-platform posts with Groq and Upload-Post

### How it works

This workflow takes a URL or raw text via webhook, runs a two-stage AI pipeline to analyze the content and generate platform-optimized posts for Instagram, Twitter, LinkedIn, email newsletter, and video script. It validates character limits for each platform, logs results to Google Sheets, posts via Upload-Post, and returns all generated content in the webhook response. Groq Llama 3.3 70B handles both AI stages on the free tier.

### What you get per run

- Instagram caption with 30 hashtags (2200 char limit)
- Twitter 5-tweet thread (280 chars each)
- LinkedIn post with professional formatting (3000 char limit)
- Email newsletter with subject, preview text, and body
- Video script with hook, main script, and CTA

## Receive and route input

Webhook accepts a JSON body with either a URL or raw text. The IF node checks whether the input contains a URL and routes to the appropriate content preparation path.

## Prepare content

URL path: fetches the page via HTTP Request and extracts clean text from the HTML response. Text path: passes the raw input directly. Both paths output a standardized content field for the AI analyzer.

## Stage 1 - Analyze content

The Content Analyzer agent reads the prepared input and extracts key themes, tone, target audience, and main points. The output is formatted as a structured content brief that feeds into the platform generator. Uses Groq Llama 3.3 70B via the Groq Llama 3.3 for analysis sub-node.

## Stage 2 - Generate and validate

The Platform Generator agent takes the content brief and produces posts for all five platforms with character-limit-aware formatting. A Code node parses the JSON output and validates that each platform field exists and meets length requirements. Failed parsing routes to the error response branch.

## Save, post, and respond

Valid results are logged to Google Sheets with timestamps and source metadata. Content is posted to connected social platforms via Upload-Post HTTP request. The final webhook response returns all generated content to the caller. Parse failures return a structured error message instead.

## Setup Guide

### Step 1 - Groq API Key (required)
1. Go to console.groq.com - API Keys - Create
2. n8n: Credentials - Add - Groq API - paste key - Save
3. Select in both the Groq Llama 3.3 for analysis and Groq Llama 3.3 for generation sub-nodes

### Step 2 - Google Sheets (required for logging)
1. n8n: Credentials - Add - Google Sheets OAuth2
2. Create a spreadsheet with columns: Timestamp, Source Type, Source, Instagram, Twitter Thread, LinkedIn, Email Subject, Email Body, Video Script, Status
3. Copy the spreadsheet URL and paste into the Log results to Google Sheets node
4. Select the correct sheet tab name

### Step 3 - Upload-Post (optional for auto-posting)
1. Sign up at upload-post.com and connect your social accounts
2. Get your API key from the Upload-Post dashboard
3. Paste the API key into the Publish via Upload-Post API node headers
4. Skip this step if you only want content generation without auto-posting

### Step 4 - Test the workflow
1. Click Test Workflow in n8n
2. Send a POST request to the webhook URL with body:
   {"url": "https://any-article-url.com"}
   or {"text": "Your content here"}
3. Check the webhook response and Google Sheets for results

## Customization

### Change platforms
Edit the Platform Generator agent prompt to add or remove platforms. For example, add Reddit, Pinterest, or TikTok script by describing the format and character limits in the prompt.

### Direct posting without Upload-Post
Replace the Publish via Upload-Post API HTTP node with native n8n nodes for Twitter, LinkedIn, or Gmail to post directly without a third-party service.

### Batch repurposing
Add a Google Sheets Trigger node that watches a sheet of URLs. Each new row triggers the workflow automatically so you can drop 50 URLs and walk away.

### Adjust AI quality
Change the Groq model from llama-3.3-70b to a smaller model for faster responses, or switch to OpenAI GPT-4o for higher quality output at a cost.
